name: hello
description: Write a function `greet(name)` that takes a name string and returns "Hello, {name}!".

name: reverse_string
description: Write a function `reverse(s)` that returns the reversed string.

name: palindrome
description: Write a function `is_palindrome(s)` that returns whether the string is a palindrome. The function should return a boolean.

name: count_vowels
description: Write a function `count_vowels(s)` that returns the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) in the string.

name: caesar_cipher
description: Write a function `caesar_encrypt(text, shift)` that shifts uppercase A-Z letters by `shift` positions, wrapping around. Non-uppercase characters are unchanged.

name: string_repeat
description: Write a function `repeat(s, n, sep)` that repeats string `s` `n` times joined by separator `sep`. 0 times returns empty string, 1 time returns just `s`.

name: fibonacci
description: Write a function `fib(n)` that returns the nth Fibonacci number. fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1.

name: factorial
description: Write a function `factorial(n)` that returns the factorial. If n is negative, return an error. Use a Result/Either pattern: return "Ok(value)" for success or "Err(message)" for failure. factorial(0)=Ok(1), factorial(-1)=Err(negative input).

name: gcd
description: Write a function `gcd(a, b)` that returns the greatest common divisor using the Euclidean algorithm.

name: is_prime
description: Write a function `is_prime(n)` that returns whether n is prime (as a boolean). 0 and 1 are not prime.
py_test:
print(str(is_prime(2)).lower())
print(str(is_prime(3)).lower())
print(str(is_prime(4)).lower())
print(str(is_prime(17)).lower())
print(str(is_prime(1)).lower())
print(str(is_prime(0)).lower())
expected:
true
true
false
true
false
false

name: collatz
description: Write a function `collatz_steps(n)` that returns the number of steps to reach 1 in the Collatz sequence. If even: n/2, if odd: 3n+1. collatz_steps(1)=0.

name: power
description: Write a function `power(base, exp)` that returns base raised to exp (non-negative integer exponent).

name: list_sum
description: Write a function `sum(lst)` that returns the sum of all integers in a list. Empty list returns 0.

name: list_filter
description: Write a function `filter(lst, pred)` that returns a new list with only elements where pred returns true. Also define `gt3(x)` returning x>3 and `is_even(x)` returning x%2==0. Print lists in [a, b, c] format.
py_test:
print(format_list(filter_list([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], gt3)))
print(format_list(filter_list([1, 2, 3], is_even)))
print(format_list(filter_list([], gt3)))

name: list_reverse
description: Write a function `reverse(lst)` that returns the reversed list. Print in [a, b, c] format.

name: list_contains
description: Write a function `contains(lst, target)` that returns whether the list contains the target (as a boolean).

name: list_zip
description: Write a function `zip(a, b)` that pairs elements from two integer lists, stopping at the shorter. Print each pair as Pair(x, y) and the list in [Pair(1, 4), ...] format.

name: list_flatten
description: Write a function `flatten(lst)` that flattens a list of lists into a single list. Print in [a, b, c] format.

name: shape_area
description: Define a Shape type with variants Circle(radius: float), Rect(width: float, height: float), Triangle(base: float, height: float). Write `area(s)` to compute the area. Use pi=3.14159. Print floats with decimal point.

name: option_unwrap
description: Write a function `unwrap_or(opt, default)` where opt is either Some(value) or None. Return the value if Some, else default.

name: result_chain
description: Write `map_result(r, addend)` that adds addend to an Ok value (propagates Err), and `and_then_divide(r, divisor)` that divides an Ok value by divisor (returns Err("division by zero") if divisor is 0, propagates Err). Use integer division. Results print as Ok(value) or Err(message).
py_test:
print(map_result(Ok(5), 10))
print(map_result(Err("fail"), 10))
print(and_then_divide(Ok(10), 2))
print(and_then_divide(Ok(10), 0))
print(and_then_divide(Err("fail"), 2))
print(and_then_divide(map_result(Ok(20), 10), 3))
expected:
Ok(15)
Err(fail)
Ok(5)
Err(division by zero)
Err(fail)
Ok(10)

name: expr_eval
description: Define an Expr type with variants Lit(n: int), Add(a: int, b: int), Mul(a: int, b: int). Write `eval(e)` that evaluates the expression and `describe(e)` that returns a string like "3 + 4" or "3 * 4" or just the number for Lit.
py_test:
print(eval_expr(Lit(5)))
print(eval_expr(Add(3, 4)))
print(eval_expr(Mul(3, 4)))
print(describe(Lit(5)))
print(describe(Add(3, 4)))
print(describe(Mul(3, 4)))

name: tree_depth
description: Define a Tree type with variants Leaf(value: int) and Node(left: Tree, right: Tree). Write `depth(t)` where Leaf has depth 1 and Node has depth 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)).
py_test:
print(depth(Leaf(1)))
print(depth(Node(Leaf(1), Leaf(2))))
print(depth(Node(Node(Leaf(1), Leaf(2)), Leaf(3))))

name: list_max
description: Write `max2(a, b)` returning the larger of two integers. Write `max_opt(a, b)` where a and b are optional integers (Some(n) or None). Returns Some(max) if at least one is Some, else None. Print as Some(value) or None.
py_test:
print(max2(3, 7))
print(max2(10, 2))
print(max2(5, 5))
print(format_opt(max_opt(Some(3), Some(7))))
print(format_opt(max_opt(Some(5), NONE)))
print(format_opt(max_opt(NONE, Some(3))))
print(format_opt(max_opt(NONE, NONE)))

name: pure_computation
description: Write a pure function `compute(x, y)` that returns (x+y) squared. Write `show_result(x)` that prints "Result: {x}".

name: io_greeting
description: Write `format_greeting(name, age)` returning "Hello, {name}! You are {age} years old." and `print_greeting(name, age)` that prints: === Greeting === on one line, then the greeting, then ================ on one line.
lm_test:
let _ = print_greeting("Alice", 30);
let _ = print_greeting("Bob", 25);
expected:
=== Greeting ===
Hello, Alice! You are 30 years old.
================
=== Greeting ===
Hello, Bob! You are 25 years old.
================

name: fizzbuzz
description: Write a function `fizzbuzz(n)` that returns "FizzBuzz" if n is divisible by both 3 and 5, "Fizz" if by 3 only, "Buzz" if by 5 only, or the number as a string otherwise.

name: safe_divide
description: Write `safe_div(a, b)` returning Ok(a/b) using integer division or Err("division by zero") if b is 0. Write `chain_div(a, b, c)` that divides a by b then the result by c, propagating errors. Print as Ok(value) or Err(message).
py_test:
print(safe_div(10, 2))
print(safe_div(10, 0))
print(safe_div(7, 2))
print(chain_div(100, 5, 4))
print(chain_div(100, 0, 4))
print(chain_div(100, 5, 0))
expected:
Ok(5)
Err(division by zero)
Ok(3)
Ok(5)
Err(division by zero)
Err(division by zero)

name: parse_and_compute
description: Write `parse_int(s)` that maps "42"->Ok(42), "10"->Ok(10), "0"->Ok(0), "7"->Ok(7), else Err("not a number: {s}"). Write `compute(a, b)` that adds two Result values (propagating first error). Write `describe_result(r)` formatting Ok as "Success: {v}" and Err as "Error: {e}". Print Results as Ok(value) or Err(message).
py_test:
print(parse_int("42"))
print(parse_int("abc"))
print(describe_result(compute(Ok(3), Ok(4))))
print(describe_result(compute(Err("bad"), Ok(4))))
print(describe_result(compute(Ok(3), Err("bad"))))
print(describe_result(compute(parse_int("42"), parse_int("10"))))
print(describe_result(compute(parse_int("42"), parse_int("abc"))))
expected:
Ok(42)
Err(not a number: abc)
Success: 7
Error: bad
Error: bad
Success: 52
Error: not a number: abc

name: validate_input
description: Write `validate_age(age)` returning Ok(age) if 0<=age<150, Err("age must be non-negative") if <0, Err("age must be under 150") if >=150. Write `validate_name(name)` returning Ok(name) if non-empty, Err("name must not be empty"). Write `validate_user(name, age)` that chains both: first validates name, then age, returns Ok("{name} (age {age})") or first error. Print as Ok(value) or Err(message).
py_test:
print(validate_age(25))
print(validate_age(-1))
print(validate_age(200))
print(validate_name("Alice"))
print(validate_name(""))
print(validate_user("Alice", 25))
print(validate_user("", 25))
print(validate_user("Alice", -1))
expected:
Ok(25)
Err(age must be non-negative)
Err(age must be under 150)
Ok(Alice)
Err(name must not be empty)
Ok(Alice (age 25))
Err(name must not be empty)
Err(age must be non-negative)
